title: Kids' Storage-Leg Desk
summary: A small desk built from two identical bookshelves standing in as legs — flip the tabletop face down, attach the shelves underneath, and the storage IS the desk base.
description:
This desk's whole trick is that the legs and the storage are the same piece: build two small bookshelves first, then attach them directly to the underside of the tabletop as the desk's base — no separate leg frame at all. Building two identical bookshelves is the entire structural task; get those square and matching, and the desk assembles itself in one final step.

The back pieces on each bookshelf can go on with simple nails and glue or pocket holes, whichever you have — but if you're using pocket holes, attach the rails first while you can still get a drill inside the shelf, then add the backs after. That sequencing note matters more than it sounds: a pocket-hole jig needs clearance to reach the joint, and a shelf with its back already on can block that access.

Final assembly is refreshingly simple: lay the tabletop face down on a flat surface, and attach the two finished bookshelves to the underside as the desk's legs. There's no separate base to build and no leg-to-top joinery beyond this one step — the storage units carry the entire desk.

materials.name: 3/4" project panel or hardwood plywood, 48 in x 24 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" project panel or hardwood plywood, 48 in x 24 in

steps.title: Build the two bookshelves
steps.body: Build two identical small bookshelves from the sides and shelves, using pocket holes if you have a jig, or 2" countersunk screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the rails
steps.body: Attach the rails to each bookshelf — nails work fine here.

steps.title: Add the back pieces
steps.body: Nail and glue the back pieces in place, or attach with pocket holes if preferred. If using pocket holes, attach the rails first so you can still fit a drill inside before the back closes off access.

steps.title: Assemble the desk
steps.body: Lay the desktop face down on a flat surface. Attach the two finished bookshelves to the underside of the desktop as the legs. Sand and finish.
materials: 3/4" project panel or hardwood plywood, 48 in x 24 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly
